title: Thyella pycnocephala
taxonomy: Thyella pycnocephala is a species of sea snail, specifically a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Pyramidellidae. It belongs to the phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, and the order Pyramidellida. Within the family Pyramidellidae, Thyella pycnocephala is part of the subfamily Thyellobinae. This classification helps to identify and categorize the species within the broader hierarchy of mollusks. The precise classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Thyella pycnocephala.

intro: Thyella pycnocephala, also known as the New Zealand prickly thistle or New Zealand thistle, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thyella pycnocephala include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as syrups and salves.
